##NAMES=Macraeite
##RRUFFID=R250078
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=K(H_2_O)Mn_2_(Fe_2_Ti)(PO_4_)_4_[O(OH)](H_2_O)_10_·4H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Cubos-Mesquitela-Mangualde pegmatite, Portugal
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Colourless to light-greenish-yellow pseudo-rhombic dodecahedral-shaped crystals found in phosphate nodules of weathered triplite, heterosite, and lithiophilite and associated with strengite, triplite, bermanite, phosphosiderite, and switzerite. Type sample, described in Grey et al (2024)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250078
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=[(H_2_O)_0.83_K_0.17_][K_0.65_(H_2_O)_0.35_](Mn^2+^_1.98_[box]_0.02_)(Fe^3+^_1.09_Al_0.31_Ti^4+^_0.52_Mg_0.08_)(Ti^4+^_0.66_Fe^3+^_0.34_)(PO_4_)_4_[O_0.87_F_0.53_(OH)_0.60_](H_2_O)_10_·4H_2_O ; Normalised to 4P and 34 (K + O + F)
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder, profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 10.56 b: 20.7 c: 12.39 alpha: 90 beta: 90.1 gamma: 90 volume: 2708 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
